sources.title: Selank, a new selective anxiolytic nootropic peptide drug: Results of clinical trials
sources.journal: Zhurnal Nevrologii i Psikhiatrii imeni S.S. Korsakova
sources.abstract: Double-blind placebo-controlled clinical trial demonstrating anxiolytic effects of Selank in patients with generalized anxiety disorder. Selank showed significant reduction in anxiety scores without sedation or cognitive impairment.

sources.title: Selank Administration Affects the Expression of Some Genes Involved in GABAergic Neurotransmission
sources.abstract: Selank modulates expression of genes encoding GABA-A receptor subunits and enzymes involved in GABA metabolism, providing molecular mechanism for anxiolytic effects.

sources.title: The effects of Selank on the morphofunctional state of the brain in rats with cerebral ischemia
sources.abstract: Study demonstrating neuroprotective effects of Selank in rat model of cerebral ischemia, showing reduced infarct volume and improved functional outcomes.

sources.title: Immunomodulatory properties of selank
sources.abstract: Review of immunomodulatory effects of Selank, demonstrating modulation of cytokine expression and enhancement of antiviral immunity in humans.

sources.title: Selank as an anxiolytic and nootropic preparation
sources.abstract: Comprehensive preclinical evaluation showing anxiolytic and nootropic effects in multiple behavioral models, establishing pharmacological profile distinct from benzodiazepines.

sources.title: Comparative anxiolytic activity of selank and buspirone
sources.journal: Bulletin of Experimental Biology and Medicine
sources.abstract: Comparative study showing Selank has anxiolytic efficacy similar to buspirone without sedative effects or motor impairment in animal behavioral tests.

sources.title: Selank and short peptides enhance expression of neuroprotective brain-derived neurotrophic factor
sources.abstract: Selank increases BDNF mRNA expression in rat hippocampus and frontal cortex, providing mechanism for nootropic and antidepressant-like effects.

sources.title: The role of the enkephalinergic system in the mechanism of action of selank
sources.journal: Bulletin of Experimental Biology and Medicine
sources.abstract: Study demonstrating Selank inhibits enkephalin-degrading enzymes, leading to increased enkephalin levels and contributing to anxiolytic effects through opioid system modulation.

sources.title: Selank peptide modulates expression of serotonin receptors of the GABAergic system
sources.abstract: Gene expression analysis showing Selank modulates serotonin receptor gene expression and interacts with GABAergic system at the transcriptional level.

sources.title: Comparative analysis of the anxiolytic effects of peptide Selank and diazepam
sources.abstract: Comparative study demonstrating Selank produces anxiolytic effects comparable to diazepam in behavioral tests but without sedation, muscle relaxation, or amnestic effects.

sources.title: Selank inhibits influenza virus-induced cytopathic effects
sources.abstract: In vitro study showing Selank inhibits influenza virus replication and cytopathic effects, supporting immunomodulatory and antiviral activity.

sources.title: Selank effects on gene expression of dopamine and serotonin receptors in rat hippocampus
sources.abstract: Study of Selank effects on dopamine and serotonin receptor gene expression, showing modulation of D1, D2, 5-HT1A, and 5-HT2A receptors in hippocampus.

sources.title: Tuftsin and selank inhibit degradation of enkephalins
sources.abstract: Both tuftsin (parent peptide) and Selank (tuftsin analogue) inhibit enkephalin-degrading enzymes in human blood, providing mechanistic insight into opioid system involvement.

sources.title: Selank: A synthetic derivative of tuftsin with nootropic and anxiolytic activity
sources.abstract: Foundational paper describing development of Selank from tuftsin, demonstrating both anxiolytic and nootropic effects across multiple preclinical models.

sources.title: Selank prevents memory impairment induced by chronic stress in rats
sources.journal: Bulletin of Experimental Biology and Medicine
sources.abstract: Selank prevents stress-induced memory deficits in rats exposed to chronic unpredictable stress, demonstrating cognitive-protective effects under stress conditions.

sources.title: The regulatory peptide Selank affects cerebral dopamine metabolism
sources.abstract: Study demonstrating Selank modulates dopamine turnover and metabolism in rat brain structures, affecting frontal cortex and striatum dopamine dynamics.

sources.title: Effects of selank on memory consolidation and modification processes in rats
sources.abstract: Selank improves memory consolidation and retrieval in rats, with effects on both short-term and long-term memory processes in behavioral paradigms.

sources.title: The anxiolytic effect of Selank
sources.journal: Zhurnal Vysshei Nervnoi Deiatelnosti imeni I.P. Pavlova
sources.abstract: Comprehensive review of Selank anxiolytic effects from the Institute of Pharmacology, covering mechanism, preclinical data, and early clinical observations.

sources.title: Comparative study of Selank and Semax in treatment of generalized anxiety disorder
sources.journal: Zhurnal Nevrologii i Psikhiatrii imeni S.S. Korsakova
sources.abstract: Randomized trial comparing Selank and Semax in GAD patients; Selank showed superior anxiolytic efficacy while Semax showed better cognitive effects.

sources.title: Effect of Selank on cognitive functions under stress in rats
sources.journal: Bulletin of Experimental Biology and Medicine
sources.abstract: Recent study confirming Selank improves cognitive performance in rats under stress conditions, with effects on attention and working memory.

sources.title: Effect of selank on the expression of cytokines by human peripheral blood cells
sources.journal: Bulletin of Experimental Biology and Medicine
sources.abstract: In vitro study of Selank effects on cytokine production by human peripheral blood mononuclear cells, demonstrating immunomodulatory activity.

sources.title: Effect of the peptide Selank on the level of inflammatory markers in the hypoxic brain
sources.abstract: Selank reduces inflammatory markers in hypoxic rat brain, suggesting anti-inflammatory neuroprotective mechanism.

sources.title: Selank effects on the catecholamine system of the brain in conditions of normal and increased anxiety
sources.abstract: Selank normalizes disturbed catecholamine (norepinephrine, dopamine) metabolism in anxiety states without affecting baseline levels.

sources.title: Molecular mechanisms of neuroprotective effects of Selank in brain ischemia
sources.abstract: Recent comprehensive review of molecular mechanisms underlying Selank neuroprotective effects, including gene expression changes and signaling pathway modulation.

sources.title: Short regulatory peptides and their effects on the expression of cytokine genes
sources.abstract: Review of Selank and related peptides' effects on cytokine gene expression, establishing molecular basis for immunomodulatory properties.

russianApproval.developer: Institute of Molecular Genetics, Russian Academy of Sciences
russianApproval.note: Approved as anxiolytic with nootropic properties; not approved outside Russia

developmentHistory.rationale: Extension stabilizes molecule against proteolysis and adds anxiolytic/nootropic activity
developmentHistory.developedBy: Institute of Molecular Genetics (IMG) and Institute of Pharmacology, Russian Academy of Sciences

evidenceSummary.regulatoryStatus: Approved in Russia (2009); not approved elsewhere
evidenceSummary.keyStrength: Extensive Russian clinical trials and 15+ years post-marketing experience; unique non-sedating anxiolytic profile
evidenceSummary.keyLimitation: Most research from Russian institutions; limited independent Western replication; no FDA/EMA trials
